name: Pentalath
author: Cameron Browne (2009) / AGP port
description: Five-in-a-row with Go-style capture, on a hexagonal board of 61 hexes (5 per side). Place a stone of your colour on an empty cell; then remove any enemy group left with no freedom (no adjacent empty cell) — the board edge grants no freedom. Suicide is illegal, but a placement may create its own freedom by capturing. Win by making a line of five or more of your stones along one of the three hex axes; captures can break up lines, which is the point. Designed by Cameron Browne's evolutionary game-design program (originally Ndengrod).
